titleStmt.title: SII 28.341: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 341.

summary.p: On a ruined temple of Narasiṁhasvāmin at the foot of the hill.

p.hi: Gaṁna-nṛipati Śaka 1399 (nidhi-randhra-vahni -śaśī) Hēmalamba, Kārttika śu. 10, Thursday = 1477 A.D., October 16.

summary.p: This inscription contains a strings of verses in Sanskrit language and Telugu script. After giving the pedigree of one Gaṁna-nṛipati, the Gadā-kshmāpati, who belonged to the fourth varṇa (caste) and whose family is stated to have consisted of warriors, records the construction of the temple of Lakshmī-Nṛisiṁha with a lofty maṇṭapa and other appurtenances, at Vinikoṇḍa, in the middle territory to the north-east of Śrīśaila.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīlakṣmīpati pādasadmajanito ¶va
rṇaśchatu rthōbhavattasminnamanāyakaḥ
namajani śrīsāgivaṁśōdbhavaḥ . ta
tvatraḥ pedagaṁna bhūtalapati sta
tyaṁbhanastippabhūpālōgāda
vibhuśca ceṁnnanṛpatiḥ kīrti pratāpā
nyitāḥ tēṣu gādakṣamā pālaḥ
śāryadhtēryayaśōnidhiḥ tasmāttippāṁbikā
garbhē jātaṁ putracatuṣṭayaṁ pratyartha
jētā pedatippabhūpaḥ pratāpavān ga
ṁnamanāyakēṁdraḥ liṁgakṣamābhṛ
tkanikāmādhēnu rguṇāṁburāśiḥ pinatippa
bhūpaḥ puliyamārkōlugaṁḍaprakaṭi
takaravālabhairavāṭōpaḥ gaṁḍabhēruṁḍa
birudaḥ karṇāṁśastēṣu gaṁnabhūpālaḥ .
dēpagēhakpativipranivāhān bhāsurō
pavana pūṇataṭākān . putramitrasavā
tōbahu kṛtvā bhāgyavān jayati
gaṁnanpapālaḥ śrīśaila pūrvōttara
madyadōśō tēna praśastī vinikōṁḍanāmyi
pure mahāmaṁṭapaśṛṁgaramyaṁ
pratiṣṭhitaṁ śrīnarasiṁhagēhaṁ .Read pū¨rṇa
śaukauddebde nidhiraṁdhravaṁ hniśa
śinaṁ khyāṁteRead saṁkhyātēsamevāmalaṁ
byākhyē kārtikaśuklapakṣadaśamā ṣu
srē gurōrvā sarē gādakṣmāpati sā
gigaṁnanṛpatirlakṣmī nṛṁsiṁhālayaṁThe anusvara is written in the next line.
sāṁgaṁ kāritavānasau nipasatā
mācaṁdra mābhāskaraṁ . maṁgaḷamahā
śrī śrī śrīA Fortice with the head to the proper right at the end of the inscription.
Digital edition of SII 28.341 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
